title: Add SOUL.md with updated protocol rules from Phase 4
head: branches/20260326_athena-protocol-update
body:
## Protocol Evolution: Codifying Phase 3 Lessons

**Problem:** Phase 3 suffered from branch contamination and CI violations because protocol wasn't explicit enough.

**Solution:** Updated SOUL.md with new UNBREAKABLE rules based on lessons learned.

### New UNBREAKABLE Rules Added:

#### 1. Workspace Isolation (UNBREAKABLE)
- **NEVER work in `omni-llm/` directly** — that's Emmanouil's clone
- **Every agent task gets a git worktree** under `omni-llm-worktrees/branches/`
- **Use `create-worktree.sh`** to create isolated workspaces
- **Always branch from `origin/main`** — never from an existing feature branch
- **Clean up worktrees** after PR merge/close: `git worktree remove <path>`
- **One agent per worktree** — never share worktrees between agents

#### 2. CI Gate (UNBREAKABLE)
- **NO MERGE WITHOUT GREEN CI. Period.**
- Before opening a PR: run `ruff check . && mypy src/omni --ignore-missing-imports && pytest tests/ -v` locally
- PR must show green checks before Emmanouil reviews
- If CI fails: fix on the same branch, push, wait for green
- **This rule exists because we learned the hard way:** merging red CI caused cascading failures

#### 3. Clean Branch Requirements
- **Always reset to origin/main** before starting work (via worktree)
- **No "fix on fix" chains** — if your branch has 3+ "fix:" commits, consider starting fresh
- **Squash when messy** — clean commit history aids review

#### 4. Pre-PR Verification (MANDATORY)
Before opening a PR, ALL must pass:
- [ ] `ruff check .` — zero errors
- [ ] `mypy src/omni --ignore-missing-imports` — zero errors
- [ ] `pytest tests/ -v` — all pass
- [ ] Branch is based on latest `origin/main`
- [ ] Worktree is clean (`git status` shows clean)

### Why These Rules Matter:

#### **Prevents Branch Contamination:**
Phase 3 suffered because agents worked sequentially in same workspace. Workspace isolation prevents this.

#### **Enforces CI Quality Gate:**
We learned the hard way: merging without green CI causes cascading failures. This rule prevents future violations.

#### **Standardizes Process:**
Clear, explicit rules eliminate ambiguity and ensure all agents follow the same protocol.

#### **Supports Sustainable Workflow:**
Protocol evolves based on lessons learned, creating a sustainable workflow for future development.

### Existing Rules Preserved:
- All original Git & Merge Workflow rules remain
- Role → Model mapping mandatory
- Branch naming sacred
- Review process mandatory
- Only Emmanouil merges

**This protocol update completes Phase 4 by codifying the lessons learned from Phase 3 into explicit, UNBREAKABLE rules.**
